verdict.note: Re-measured at HEAD via a fresh 'spex init' throwaway project (isolated SPEXCODE_HOME, worktree CLI): rendered hooks-manifest is byte-exact the legacy map — Notification→idle, PostToolUse→spec-of-file, PreToolUse→mark-active(10)+spec-first(20,block), Stop→stop-gate(block), StopFailure→session-fail, UserPromptSubmit→mark-active; one TAB-line per node×event, sorted by event then order.

All four edit sources (editor rewrite, echo >>, sed -i, new node file) each moved the content hash and re-rendered the manifest exactly once; a bare touch moved nothing. Gate stayed pure-shell on the unchanged path (~80ms CPU under load-32 box; node booted only on real changes).

spec-first's {decision:block} stdout passed through the dispatcher UNCHANGED; dispatch exited 2 per the governing spec (ddc8a01: JSON decision from a block:true handler raises the exit — the yatsu expected's stale '(exit 0)' was folded to match). mark-active's side effect landed regardless (idle→active, stale proposal+note cleared) — all handlers ran; second access passed silently on the sentinel.

Two concurrent dispatches after a .config edit: exactly ONE materialize ran (SPEX-wrapper counter), the second saw the fresh hash inside the mkdir lock re-check and skipped; manifest intact after (7 lines, 4 TAB fields each, sorted, hash current).

Re-measured at 72186b22 via real dispatch.sh in a throwaway spex-init project (isolated homes): spec-first's {decision:block} stdout passed through byte-identical, dispatch exited 2, mark-active's side effect (idle→active, stale proposal+note cleared) landed regardless — all handlers ran.

reproduced on main HEAD: the manifest is one global per-project file, so tree A's later materialize (stock .config) overwrites the compile tree B's dispatch reads — B's own SessionStart hook (probe-b) silently vanishes from B's dispatch (cross-tree hook bleed; root-cause residue of the 6c02 race)

note: mis-anchored: filed while yatsu.md was uncommitted; re-filing against the clean HEAD that carries the scenario

verdict.note: reproduced: the manifest is one global per-project file, so tree A's later materialize (stock .config) overwrites the compile tree B's dispatch reads — B's own SessionStart hook (probe-b) silently vanishes from B's dispatch (cross-tree hook bleed; 6c02 race root-cause residue)

fixed: each tree's materialize writes trees/<enc(toplevel)>/hooks-manifest in the global store; dispatch derives the slot from its own cwd — tree A's later render lands in A's slot and B's dispatch fires B's own hook set (PROBE-B)

slot-less tree dispatches off the legacy global manifest (hook fires, no silent no-op); after spex materialize plants the slot, the slot shadows the legacy file (which stays as never-rewritten residue for uninstall)
